fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to arkansas, speech-language pathologist compact arkansas, arkansas speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201
board.physical_address: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Arkansas.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision.

steps.title: Meet the Education Requirements
steps.description: You must hold a master's degree or its equivalent in speech-language pathology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience that meets the requirements outlined by the Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Exam and Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Successfully pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology and submit a complete application to the Board, including all required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Arkansas issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Arkansas is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arkansas through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Arkansas SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $100. Annual renewal is $75. Note that Arkansas requires annual rather than biennial renewal.

faqs.question: Is Arkansas part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Arkansas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Arkansas is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arkansas through a compact privilege.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Arkansas
hero.dek: Arkansas Speech-Language Pathologist License requires a master's degree in speech-language pathology, passing the Praxis exam, and completing a supervised professional experience.
